summary: Analyst completed EXP-211 (WIN-038 detail_evidence argument (iv) parenthetical engaging the H(r)-specific coupled radial-vertical eigenvalue escape route) per origin tracker entry. Patch inserts ~4-sentence parenthetical into paragraph 4, between "globe fingerprint is imported a second time." and "There is no mode formula...". Acknowledges kernel-of-truth (the honest calculation IS a coupled eigenvalue problem on dome-specific H(r)) then closes the escape via (a) radial boundary a=20,015 km=pi*R_Earth globe-fingerprint import and (b) Courant-Faber-Krahn domain-containment lower bound => fundamental TM-like mode >= 5.73 Hz. Addresses c6 hole 1 (ISS-1341). Holes 2-7 explicitly deferred as out-of-scope.

collision_alert.description: At analyst run start, the origin tracker (fresh clone) showed EXP-211 = WIN-038 coupled-eigenvalue task (pending, created 2026-04-21T22:34:39Z by decider). But the workspace (FUSE-mounted) tracker shows EXP-211 = design-refresh-phase-a accuracy-math sidebar demotion (status complete, created 2026-04-21T23:09:59Z from HNOTE-DESIGN-REFRESH-PHASE-A-001). The workspace EXP-211 was never synced to origin (presumably workspace-sync has not yet run since the prior analyst claimed the ID ~3 hours before this run). Two DIFFERENT tasks share the same EXP ID. workspace-sync on its next run will encounter a tracker-level rebase conflict because the tracker is multi-writer / unclassified.
collision_alert.data_loss: When this analyst run wrote monitor/analyst/expansions/EXP-211.json in the workspace with the WIN-038 content, it OVERWROTE the prior design-refresh EXP-211.json content (which the prior analyst had written for Phase A Item 4). FUSE does not support unlink, and no pre-write backup was taken. The design-refresh JSON content cannot be recovered from this workspace; the accompanying proposal file (monitor/analyst/issue-proposals/proposal-EXP-211-draft-ready.json) summarizes the intent but does not include the full replacement_html. Alerting so the operator / Claude Design run can redo that work under a fresh EXP ID.
affected_files: monitor/analyst/expansions/EXP-211.json (workspace: design-refresh content overwritten, now WIN-038 content)
monitor/analyst/expansion-tracker.json (workspace: still references design-refresh EXP-211; origin: WIN-038 EXP-211 pending; UNRESOLVED conflict)
monitor/analyst/issue-proposals/proposal-EXP-211-draft-ready.json (workspace: references the overwritten file — contents no longer match)
recommended_remediation: Decider / tinker / operator: allocate a fresh EXP ID (e.g., EXP-213) for the design-refresh accuracy-math work and move the workspace tracker design-refresh entry to the new ID.
Instruct the prior analyst session (or Claude Design) to re-run the Phase A Item 4 accuracy-math sidebar demotion under the new EXP ID.
Mark the overwritten proposal-EXP-211-draft-ready.json as superseded; create a fresh proposal under the new EXP ID.

Current analyst session preserved a disambiguated copy at monitor/analyst/expansions/EXP-211-WIN038-coupled-eigenvalue.json so the WIN-038 work is recoverable if the primary EXP-211.json gets squashed during conflict resolution.

Root cause for tinker to address: EXP-ID allocation is not atomic across concurrent workspace + decider-clone writers. PROP-009-style collision-proof allocation (pop-time strict matching) for EXP IDs would prevent recurrence.

All other text byte-identical to post-EXP-204 paragraph. No TLDR changes. No verdict changes. Affects only WIN-038 detail_evidence; no cross-WIN edits.

curmudgeon_request_notes: Not needed; patch is a surgical response to c6 hole 1 and confines itself to that hole. If the decider wants adversarial review, request a c7 cycle on WIN-038 after integration.
